flashcards.front: What is a structure in C?
flashcards.back: A structure is a user-defined data type that groups variables of different data types under a single name. Each member has its own memory location.

flashcards.front: How is memory allocated for a union?
flashcards.back: A union allocates memory equal to the size of its largest member. All members share this same memory space.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between structures and unions?
flashcards.back: Structures allocate separate memory for each member, while unions share the same memory location for all members.

flashcards.front: How do you declare a structure variable?
flashcards.back: You declare a structure variable by using the structure name followed by the variable name. Example: struct Student s1;

flashcards.front: What happens when you assign a value to one member of a union?
flashcards.back: Assigning a value to one member of a union overwrites the memory shared by all members, making the values of other members undefined.
